methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Rainbow Robotics, a robotics and manufacturing company founded by Oh Jun-ho, was acquired after raising $50M+. The valuation analysis is constrained by a limited peer universe: one public comparable (ticker 277810.KQ) and eight private peers including 1X Technologies, Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, and Bedrock Robotics. The broader robotics and manufacturing sector contains eleven relevant public and private companies, but the acquired stage and specific valuation tier of Rainbow Robotics create narrow comparability.

The peer-median approach is not applicable due to insufficient same-stage and adjacent-stage matches. A filtering protocol requiring at least three comparables of aligned stage and valuation tier yielded zero qualifying datapoints. Applying an all-sector median would introduce material distortion given the heterogeneity of robotics subsegments, manufacturing verticals, and capital intensity across the peer set. Without defensible triangulation via revenue or EBITDA multiples, transaction multiples, or DCF anchors, no single valuation method produces reliable output.

The analysis supports only a qualitative range: the acquisition price likely reflects a premium to the $50M+ raised, consistent with acquisition dynamics, but cannot be quantified without transaction terms. Key uncertainties include: the timing and method of exit (strategic vs. financial buyer), the robotics subsegment's growth trajectory relative to public comparables, and whether any peer trading or funding data approximates Rainbow's operational profile. A credible valuation would require operational metrics or buyer identity.
